name: U.S. Open
stakes: This week is a crucial test for Rounak Chouhan to continue his dream run and for India to potentially unearth a new star on the Super 300 circuit.
summary:
India's campaign at the U.S. Open has seen a significant breakthrough with unranked Rounak Chouhan making waves in the Men's Singles draw. Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, pulled off a stunning upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen, demonstrating remarkable grit and skill. This victory has propelled him into the quarterfinals, where he is set to face Israel's Misha Zilberman.

Chouhan's unexpected run is the highlight for the Indian contingent, especially as other prominent Indian shuttlers were not mentioned in recent reports regarding their progress in the tournament. His performance underscores the depth of emerging talent within Indian badminton, offering a glimpse of future potential on the international stage.

indiaContingent.name: Rounak Chouhan
indiaContingent.note: Stunned World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen
